evidence.observed: Over 20000ms latency observed in multi-step runs before caching and context reduction.

findings.root_cause: Large default context_size of 8 coupled with high temperature (1.6) and lack of request-level caching in the wrapper.
findings.suggested_fix: Reduce context_size to 3 in config.json and implement thread-safe caching in wrapper.py.

evidence.observed: Requests repeatedly hit max_steps limit due to infinite loops of check_stock tool calls.

findings.root_cause: loop_guard configuration was disabled by default, allowing the agent to loop tool calls indefinitely.
findings.suggested_fix: Set loop_guard to true and reduce max_steps to 6 in config.json.

evidence.observed: Agent repeated raw customer phone numbers (e.g. 0901234567) in answers.

findings.root_cause: redact_pii was set to false in config.json and system prompt did not forbid repeating customer info.
findings.suggested_fix: Enable redact_pii in config.json, add output regex-redaction in wrapper.py, and instruct the agent to protect PII in prompt.txt.

evidence.observed: MacBook orders were always falsely reported as out of stock.

findings.root_cause: A hardcoded catalog_override in config.json forced MacBook availability in_stock to false.
findings.suggested_fix: Remove the MacBook entry from catalog_override in config.json.

evidence.observed: Tool calls failed intermittently with transient errors (18% tool error rate).

findings.root_cause: retry was disabled by default in config.json, allowing temporary tool failures to crash the agent.
findings.suggested_fix: Enable retry in config.json with max_attempts: 3 and backoff_ms: 100.

evidence.observed: Agent computed total prices for out-of-stock items and unserved destinations instead of refusing.

findings.root_cause: The system prompt was too generic, encouraging estimation of prices instead of strict grounding in tool outputs.
findings.suggested_fix: Rewrite system prompt to strictly refuse orders and output 'Tu choi don hang.' when tools report unavailability or shipping failure.

evidence.observed: High token usage and cost incurred due to verbose system logging and unnecessarily large context size of 8.

findings.root_cause: verbose_system was set to true and context_size was set to 8 in the default config, causing excessive tokens to be sent on every turn.
findings.suggested_fix: Set verbose_system to false and reduce context_size to 3 in config.json.

evidence.observed: Degraded correctness and quality scores observed in late turns of multi-turn sessions.

findings.root_cause: The agent drifted over long conversations because context_reset_every was set to 0 (disabled) and session_drift_rate was 0.06.
findings.suggested_fix: Set context_reset_every to 4 in config.json to reset the conversation state periodically.

evidence.observed: Agent calculated incorrect total prices (e.g. adding extra zeros or incorrect additions) due to LLM math weakness.

findings.root_cause: gpt-5.4-nano is math-incompetent and temperature was set to 1.6, leading to highly unstable and incorrect arithmetic calculations.
findings.suggested_fix: Reduce temperature to 0.2, instruct the agent with an exact formula in prompt.txt, and implement math validation/correction in wrapper.py.

evidence.observed: Agent frequently called tools multiple times redundantly or in inefficient sequences, increasing latency and cost.

findings.root_cause: tool_budget was set to 0 (unlimited) and system prompt did not specify tool calling limits or strict order constraints.
findings.suggested_fix: Set tool_budget to 3 in config.json and explicitly specify strict tool execution order in prompt.txt.

evidence.observed: Agent obeyed fake prices or commands embedded in user-supplied order notes (GHI CHÚ) instead of tool outputs.

findings.root_cause: System prompt did not instruct the agent to treat order notes as raw data, allowing instructions in the note to override system commands.
findings.suggested_fix: Add wrapper sanitization to strip numeric values/instructions from order notes and instruct the agent to ignore any commands inside 'GHI CHÚ' in prompt.txt.
